HomeAbout

About Us

Our story, mission, and the driving force behind Bugwere Coffee Company.

Our Story

Founded on a Simple Question

Founded in 2023, Bugwere Coffee Company (BCC) was established to respond to a critical challenge: How can rural households with limited land still generate sustainable income?

Our answer lies in a holistic, community-driven approach that combines high-value coffee and cocoa production with diversified income streams, expert agronomic support, and guaranteed market access. We believe that every household, regardless of land size, deserves the opportunity to build a prosperous and sustainable future.

Meet Our Team

5,000+

Families empowered

Our Vision

“To transform Uganda's rural farming communities into empowered, sustainable producers and exporters of high-quality coffee.”

Our Mission

To empower rural households through a complete, end-to-end agricultural support system that provides quality seedlings, expert training, guaranteed markets, and financial inclusion, enabling families to build lasting prosperity regardless of land size.

What We Stand For

Our Values

Community First

Every decision we make centers on the well-being and prosperity of the farming communities we serve.

Innovation

We embrace new approaches and technologies that can amplify our impact and transform rural agriculture.

Accountability

We measure our success by the real, tangible improvements in farmers' lives, not just numbers on a page.

Partnership

We believe lasting change comes from collaboration — with communities, governments, and organizations.

Sustainability

Our model is designed to create self-sustaining ecosystems that outlast any single program or intervention.

Our Journey

Milestones

23

2023

The Beginning

Bugwere Coffee Company was founded to address the critical challenge of sustainable income for rural households with limited land in Eastern Uganda.

23

2023

First Seedlings

Launched our coffee seedling distribution program, reaching the first 1,000 homes in the Bugwere region with high-quality coffee plantlets.

24

2024

Cocoa Expansion

Expanded into cocoa farming, diversifying income streams for farming families and strengthening economic resilience across the community.

24

2024

7,000+ Homes

Our fertilizer distribution program scaled rapidly, reaching over 7,000 households and dramatically improving crop yields across the region.

25

2025

Growing Impact

Today, BCC touches over 15,000 lives through integrated programs spanning coffee, cocoa, livestock, agronomy, and community development.

Governance & Oversight

Board of Directors

Our board brings together distinguished professionals from agriculture, finance, governance, and community development — united by a commitment to BCC's mission and the communities we serve.

DJ

Dr. James Mulondo

Board Chairperson

Seasoned development economist with over 25 years of experience in agricultural policy and rural transformation across East Africa. Former advisor to the Ministry of Agriculture.

GN

Grace Nabwire

Vice Chairperson

Social entrepreneur and women's empowerment advocate. Founder of a regional cooperative network that connects over 3,000 smallholder farmers to premium markets.

PW

Peter Wamboga

Board Secretary

Corporate governance specialist and legal advisor with extensive experience in non-profit board administration and compliance within Uganda's cooperative sector.

SA

Sarah Achieng

Treasurer

Chartered accountant and microfinance expert. Brings 18 years of financial oversight experience in agricultural value chains and community lending institutions.

DR

Dr. Robert Ochieng

Board Member

Agronomist and researcher specializing in climate-smart agriculture. Published author on sustainable coffee farming practices in the Lake Victoria crescent.

MN

Monica Nantale

Board Member

Community development practitioner with deep roots in the Bugwere sub-region. Champions inclusive programming that ensures women and youth participation.

DK

David Kisakye

Board Member

Supply chain and export logistics expert. Previously led coffee export operations for a leading Ugandan trading house, connecting farmers to international buyers.

FI

Florence Ikiriza

Board Member

Education and capacity building specialist. Designs training curricula for farmer field schools and adult learning programs in rural agricultural communities.

Meet the Team Behind BCC

Our leadership brings expertise, dedication, and a shared commitment to transforming communities.
